feature: PowerUp Drop System - Probabilistic item drops from destroyed bricks
workflow_rationale: New game feature that requires creating new files (PowerUpType, PowerUpItem, PowerUpDropManager), modifying existing infrastructure (ActionId/payload, Brick.cs, BrickGameManager.cs), and wiring them together. Single service (Unity) but multi-layer (infrastructure, controllers, managers).

phases.name: Event Infrastructure
phases.description: Enhance the ActionBus event system to support brick destruction events with position data, and publish the event from Brick.cs. This is the foundation that PowerUpDropManager will subscribe to.

subtasks.description: Enhance BrickGameBrickDestroyedPayload with Position and OwnerClientId fields, and add BrickGame_PowerUpCollected ActionId to the ActionId enum in IAction.cs

changes: Add Vector3 Position and ulong OwnerClientId to BrickGameBrickDestroyedPayload struct (add 'using UnityEngine;' at top for Vector3)
Add BrickGame_PowerUpCollected to ActionId enum (for future use when powerup is picked up)
Create BrickGamePowerUpCollectedPayload readonly struct with PowerUpType field (int for now since enum not yet created)
important_notes: BrickGameBrickDestroyedPayload is readonly struct : IActionPayload
Follow existing pattern: constructor with all fields, get-only properties
Vector3 needs UnityEngine namespace import at top of file

verification.instructions: Verify IAction.cs compiles by checking: 1) BrickGameBrickDestroyedPayload has Position (Vector3) and OwnerClientId (ulong) properties. 2) BrickGame_PowerUpCollected ActionId exists. 3) No syntax errors.

subtasks.notes: Enhanced BrickGameBrickDestroyedPayload with Position (Vector3) and OwnerClientId (ulong) fields. Added BrickGame_PowerUpCollected ActionId to the enum. Added using UnityEngine for Vector3. Commit: e7f6779

subtasks.description: Modify Brick.cs to publish BrickGame_BrickDestroyed event with enhanced payload (position + ownerClientId) when a brick is destroyed, in both HandleBallCollision and TakeDamage flows

changes: Add 'using MB.Infrastructure.Messages;' import at top
In HandleBrickDestruction(), add: Managers.PublishAction(ActionId.BrickGame_BrickDestroyed, new BrickGameBrickDestroyedPayload(originalWave, transform.position, OwnerClientId));
The position is transform.position of the brick being destroyed
OwnerClientId comes from the existing public property on Brick
important_notes: HandleBrickDestruction() is called from both HandleBallCollision() and TakeDamage() - only need to add the publish in HandleBrickDestruction() since both flows call it
This also fixes the existing issue where BrickGame_BrickDestroyed was never published despite BrickGameSoundBinder subscribing to it
Server authority: Brick collision is already server-authoritative (IsServer check in PhysicsObject.OnCollisionEnter2D)

verification.instructions: Verify Brick.cs: 1) Has MB.Infrastructure.Messages import. 2) HandleBrickDestruction() calls Managers.PublishAction with BrickGame_BrickDestroyed and enhanced payload. 3) No syntax errors.

subtasks.notes: Added MB.Infrastructure.Messages import and Managers.PublishAction(ActionId.BrickGame_BrickDestroyed, new BrickGameBrickDestroyedPayload(originalWave, transform.position, OwnerClientId)) call in HandleBrickDestruction(). Both HandleBallCollision and TakeDamage flows publish the event since both call HandleBrickDestruction(). Commit: 013880c

phases.name: PowerUp Core Types
phases.description: Create the PowerUpType enum and PowerUpItem NetworkBehaviour - the core types for the drop system.

subtasks.description: Create PowerUpType.cs enum defining Star and BonusBall powerup types in the BrickGame PowerUp directory

changes: Create enum PowerUpType { None = 0, Star = 1, BonusBall = 2 }
Place in namespace or global scope matching project convention (global like GamePhase)

verification.instructions: Verify PowerUpType.cs: 1) Enum exists with None, Star, BonusBall values. 2) File is in correct directory. 3) No syntax errors.

subtasks.notes: Created PowerUpType.cs enum with None=0, Star=1, BonusBall=2 values in Assets/@Scripts/Contents/BrickGame/PowerUp/ directory. Follows project convention (global scope, XML doc comments matching GamePhase.cs pattern). Commit: ba1c4db

subtasks.description: Create PowerUpItem.cs - a PhysicsObject (NetworkBehaviour) that falls with gravity, detects Plank collision for pickup and BottomBoundary for despawn, and applies effects (Star: power boost, BonusBall: extra ball)

changes: Extend PhysicsObject (same as Star/BonusBall)
Fields: PowerUpType _powerUpType, bool isCollected, float fallSpeed = 3f
Public Initialize(PowerUpType type) method to set type after instantiation
Override OnTriggerEnter2D for Plank collision (tag 'Plank' or PhysicsPlank component)
Also check for 'BottomBoundary' tag in OnTriggerEnter2D for despawn
HandlePlankCollision(): apply effect based on powerup type, then despawn
Star effect: find balls owned by same player, call ball.PowerUp(1, 15f)
BonusBall effect: spawn extra ball at plank position (follow BonusBall.cs pattern)
Despawn pattern: StartCoroutine(DestroyAfterDelay(0.1f)) same as Star.cs
FixedUpdate: apply constant downward velocity if server (or set gravity via Rigidbody2D)
Visual: SpriteRenderer with color-coding (yellow for Star, green for BonusBall)
Set isTrigger = true on collider so it doesn't bounce off plank/walls
important_notes: This is DIFFERENT from existing Star/BonusBall: PowerUpItem falls to PLANK, not caught by Ball
Server authority: OnTriggerEnter2D checks if (!IsServer && IsSpawned) return;
Must use namespace Unity.Assets.Scripts.Objects to match Star.cs/BonusBall.cs
Ownership: check if plank's OwnerClientId matches powerup's OwnerClientId for multiplayer
BottomBoundary despawn: if collision with BottomBoundary tag, despawn without effect

verification.instructions: Verify PowerUpItem.cs: 1) Extends PhysicsObject. 2) Has Initialize(PowerUpType) method. 3) OnTriggerEnter2D handles Plank pickup and BottomBoundary despawn. 4) Applies correct effect per type. 5) Server authority checks present. 6) No syntax errors.

subtasks.notes: Created PowerUpItem.cs extending PhysicsObject in namespace Unity.Assets.Scripts.Objects. Key implementation: Initialize(PowerUpType, ownerClientId) method, OnTriggerEnter2D handles Plank pickup (with multiplayer ownership check) and BottomBoundary despawn, Star effect calls ball.PowerUp() on same-owner balls, BonusBall effect spawns extra ball at plank position (follows BonusBall.cs pattern). Server authority checks: if (!IsServer && IsSpawned) return in OnTriggerEnter2D, HasAuthorityToModifyPhysics() in FixedUpdate, NetworkManager.Singleton.IsServer for spawn. Visual: SpriteRenderer color-coding (yellow=Star, green=BonusBall). isTrigger=true, constant fallSpeed=3f via rb.linearVelocity. Commit: c2c9bdd

phases.name: Drop Manager & Wiring
phases.description: Create the PowerUpDropManager POCO class and wire it into BrickGameManager as a sub-manager.

subtasks.description: Create PowerUpDropManager.cs - POCO class that subscribes to BrickGame_BrickDestroyed events, rolls probability table, and spawns PowerUpItem NetworkObjects on the server

changes: POCO class (NOT MonoBehaviour) following BallManager pattern
Probability table: Star 5% (0.05f), BonusBall 3% (0.03f), None 92%
Initialize() method: subscribe to BrickGame_BrickDestroyed via ActionBus, create runtime prefab
CreatePowerUpPrefab(): runtime prefab creation following BrickGameBulletSpawner.CreateBulletPrefab() pattern
OnBrickDestroyed(ActionMessage): extract position+owner from payload, roll probability, spawn if hit
SpawnPowerUpItem(Vector3 position, PowerUpType type, ulong ownerClientId): Instantiate prefab, configure, Spawn
Server authority check: MultiplayerUtil.HasServerAuthority() before spawning
Dispose() method: unsubscribe from ActionBus
Store IDisposable for subscription cleanup
Register prefab with NetworkManager.Singleton.AddNetworkPrefab()
important_notes: POCO class - NO MonoBehaviour inheritance
Use UnityEngine.Object.Instantiate() (static method works from POCO)
Runtime prefab: create GameObject with PowerUpItem + NetworkObject + SpriteRenderer + Rigidbody2D + CircleCollider2D(isTrigger=true)
Prefab Rigidbody2D: gravityScale = 1 (or use constant velocity in PowerUpItem.FixedUpdate)
Color-code prefab based on PowerUpType at spawn time
Random.value for probability roll (Unity's Random is accessible from POCO)
Must handle case where NetworkManager.Singleton is null (singleplayer)

verification.instructions: Verify PowerUpDropManager.cs: 1) Is POCO (no MonoBehaviour). 2) Has Initialize()/Dispose() lifecycle. 3) Subscribes to BrickGame_BrickDestroyed. 4) Probability table: Star 5%, BonusBall 3%. 5) Creates runtime prefab. 6) Server authority check before spawn. 7) No syntax errors.

subtasks.notes: Created PowerUpDropManager.cs as a POCO class (no MonoBehaviour) following BallManager pattern. Key implementation: Initialize()/Dispose() lifecycle, subscribes to BrickGame_BrickDestroyed via ActionBus, probability table (Star 5%, BonusBall 3%, None 92%), creates runtime prefab with PowerUpItem + NetworkObject + Rigidbody2D + CircleCollider2D(isTrigger) + SpriteRenderer, server authority check via MultiplayerUtil.HasServerAuthority(), IDisposable subscription cleanup, registers prefab with NetworkManager.Singleton.AddNetworkPrefab(). Commit: 40ec9d6

subtasks.description: Wire PowerUpDropManager into BrickGameManager.cs as a sub-manager - add field, constructor instantiation, Initialize/Dispose calls, and public property accessor

changes: Add private PowerUpDropManager _powerUpDropManager; field in Sub-Managers region
Add public PowerUpDropManager PowerUpDrop => _powerUpDropManager; property accessor
In constructor: _powerUpDropManager = new PowerUpDropManager();
In Initialize(): _powerUpDropManager.Initialize();
In StartGame(): (no additional init needed since Initialize is separate)
Consider cleanup in GameOver or when game ends: _powerUpDropManager may need no special cleanup beyond ActionBus unsubscribe
important_notes: Follow exact same pattern as _ballManager, _brickManager, _plankManager
PowerUpDropManager.Initialize() subscribes to ActionBus, so it must be called after Managers is ready
Access pattern: Managers.Game.BrickGame.PowerUpDrop

verification.instructions: Verify BrickGameManager.cs: 1) Has _powerUpDropManager field. 2) Has public PowerUpDrop property. 3) Constructor creates new PowerUpDropManager(). 4) Initialize() calls _powerUpDropManager.Initialize(). 5) No syntax errors.

subtasks.notes: Wired PowerUpDropManager into BrickGameManager.cs following existing sub-manager pattern (PlankManager, BallManager, BrickManager). Added: 1) private PowerUpDropManager _powerUpDropManager field, 2) public PowerUpDropManager PowerUpDrop property accessor, 3) Constructor instantiation: new PowerUpDropManager(), 4) Initialize() call: _powerUpDropManager.Initialize(). Access pattern: Managers.Game.BrickGame.PowerUpDrop. Commit: e7fa0a4

phases.name: Compilation & Integration Validation
phases.description: Verify all files compile together and the powerup drop system is correctly wired end-to-end.

subtasks.description: Verify complete integration: all new and modified files compile, event flow is correct (Brick -> ActionBus -> PowerUpDropManager -> PowerUpItem -> Plank), and no circular dependencies exist

checks: All 3 new files exist: PowerUpType.cs, PowerUpItem.cs, PowerUpDropManager.cs
All 3 modified files updated: IAction.cs, Brick.cs, BrickGameManager.cs

Event flow: Brick.HandleBrickDestruction() -> PublishAction(BrickGame_BrickDestroyed, payload with position) -> PowerUpDropManager.OnBrickDestroyed() -> probability roll -> SpawnPowerUpItem() -> PowerUpItem falls -> OnTriggerEnter2D(Plank) -> effect applied
No namespace conflicts or missing imports
Server authority maintained throughout the flow
Multiplayer ownership correctly tracked (ownerClientId passed from brick to powerup item)

steps: Review all new files for compilation errors
Trace event flow from Brick destruction to PowerUpItem effect application
Verify server authority at each step
Check that PowerUpItem despawns on BottomBoundary
Ensure no MonoBehaviour on PowerUpDropManager (POCO requirement)

subtasks.notes: Integration validation complete. All 6 files verified (3 new: PowerUpType.cs, PowerUpItem.cs, PowerUpDropManager.cs; 3 modified: IAction.cs, Brick.cs, BrickGameManager.cs). Event flow traced end-to-end: Brick -> ActionBus -> PowerUpDropManager -> PowerUpItem -> Plank/BottomBoundary. Server authority verified at every step. Multiplayer ownership chain verified: Brick.OwnerClientId -> Payload -> PowerUpDropManager -> PowerUpItem._ownerClientId -> collision checks. PowerUpDropManager confirmed POCO (no MonoBehaviour). No circular dependencies. No compilation issues found. All 8 acceptance criteria checks passed.

parallelism.speedup_estimate: Sequential execution - single service, file dependencies between phases

acceptance_criteria: All new and modified files compile without errors (0 errors)
Brick destruction publishes BrickGame_BrickDestroyed event with position and owner data
PowerUpDropManager subscribes to event and rolls probability (Star 5%, BonusBall 3%, None 92%)
PowerUpItem falls with gravity and is collected by Plank (paddle)
Star effect: increases ball attack power
Server Authority maintained for all drop decisions and spawning
Multiplayer: each player area has independent drops

verification_steps.name: Code Review - New Files
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Files follow codebase conventions, no syntax errors

verification_steps.name: Code Review - Modified Files
verification_steps.command: Review changes to IAction.cs, Brick.cs, BrickGameManager.cs
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Changes are minimal and correct, existing functionality preserved

verification_steps.name: Event Flow Trace
verification_steps.command: Trace: Brick destroyed -> ActionBus -> PowerUpDropManager -> PowerUpItem spawn -> fall -> Plank pickup -> effect
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Complete event chain with no broken links

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk - new feature with multiple new files and modifications to core systems (ActionBus, Brick.cs, BrickGameManager.cs). Unity project without CLI compilation, so manual review is the primary verification method.

checks: Server authority checks present in all relevant methods
ActionBus event published from Brick.cs with position data
PowerUpDropManager wired into BrickGameManager as sub-manager
